mentions.note: Unique position: not raising capital for AI; piggybacking on Alphabet's investment instead. WWDC Monday will detail Siri upgrade. Don't chase here.
SaaS cohort shrinking with cheaper competitors eroding pricing power. Even after thrashing, still not low enough to own; stay away.
Semiconductor design leader: sold some at 310, ran to 400, but lost discipline not selling more. Down 12% today. Parabolic moves always correct; must trim winners systematically.

Moved to NYSE (largest transfer in 234 years); $50B US manufacturing commitment. Strong oncology, weight-management opportunity, AI accelerating drug development. On track for ambitious $80B revenue target by 2030.
10-year breakout to 72 on gas turbine pivot but another parabolic pattern. At 72 could drop to 60 in heartbeat; wait for pullback before entry.
Stock at $33 down from $71 a year ago; turnaround story in progress. Would like to buy but stock needs to prove earnings strength first.
Online pet retailer trading cheap; may miss earnings like Petco. Monitor for consumer spending deterioration in pet category going forward.
Parabolic move with 100%+ YTD return. Sell half position immediately; expects this stock to get 'really smashed' in correction.
Down 22% YTD with sky-high 7.2% yield—market doubts dividend coverage. Packaged food sector needs consolidation; standalone positions face risk.
2023 spin-off from FedEx; LTL specialist with path to become one of best operators globally. Withstanding market stress well; long-term positioning strong.
Athleta athletic division down 12% in sales, 11% same-store sales. Long turnaround project; market penalizes these. At 9x earnings, no compelling upside without turnaround proof.
Diversified pharma with triple-A balance sheet (better than US government) and robust new drug pipeline. Anchoring charitable trust through tech meltdown.
Home builder needs rate cuts to stimulate demand and move homes. Higher rate environment is headwind; wait for Fed policy shift.
Volatile defense stock fundamentally sound. Buy initial tranche now at 523; add more if it pulls back below 500 for better cost basis.
Athleisure trend out of style; lost pricing power to competition (Hoka, Adidas, etc). Down 45% YTD at 10x earnings (was 30x+). Falling knife; cheaper entry likely ahead.
Beef costs elevated but dividend safe at 2.6% yield and 21x earnings. Scale purchases: 25 shares at 280, 279, 270, 265, then 50 at 260; patience rewarded.
Ski resort company rebounding of late but trajectory unclear. Expensive gas prices ($110+ fill-ups) dampen consumer travel demand and hurt backdrop.
Iconic brand in crisis: revenue down 3%, Direct down 7%, Converse down 35% (catastrophic), gross margin contracted 130bps to 40.2%. Elliott Hill turnaround moving slower than needed.
Database giant pivoted into data center business years ago. Reports Wednesday; results will clarify if AI infrastructure bet is working.
Business development company model. Won't recommend BDCs; opaque holdings and weak historical track record make them unsuitable despite headline yields.
Consumer staples leader performing well in trust during selloff. Diversified portfolio structure allows safe ownership of higher-risk tech via PG/JNJ buffer.
Aerospace/semiconductor testing equipment company parabolic after good quarter. Avoid chasing parabolic stocks; what goes up in straight line comes down faster.
Unique social media property with strong community moat. At 29x earnings, scale in purchases slowly rather than buy aggressively to manage risk.
Reports Tuesday morning repeating prior pattern: jumped on okay quarter then got smashed. Expect similar earnings-driven selloff this quarter.
Commercial real estate portfolio concentrated in weaker cities. CEO hasn't raised dividend in 12+ years. Not recommending; has not been a good stock.
Pet supply retailer missed earnings badly. Signal of consumer pullback in discretionary pet spending despite official labor market strength.
